sections.heading: Pearls
sections.content:
CUP is a heterogeneous group of metastatic tumors with an unknown primary site, accounting for 2%–9% of all tumors.2,8
The initial workup should include a core needle biopsy (preferred) or FNA with cell block, immunohistochemistry (IHC) staining, microsatellite instability (MSI) or mismatch repair (MMR) testing, HER2 IHC, and biomarker testing using next-generation sequencing (NGS).1
Treatment is tailored to the most likely primary site if identified through workup; otherwise, systemic therapy is chosen based on histologic type and biomarker status.1
For patients with disseminated disease, the preferred treatment option is enrollment in a clinical trial. Systemic therapy is generally palliative and should be limited to symptomatic patients (PS 1–2) or asymptomatic patients (PS 0) with aggressive cancer.1
Favorable prognostic subsets exist (e.g., SCC of cervical nodes, poorly differentiated carcinomas with midline distribution) and may benefit from specific, potentially curative therapies.22,26,27
Biomarker testing is crucial: dMMR/MSI-H or TMB-H tumors respond to pembrolizumab; BRAF V600E to dabrafenib/trametinib; NTRK fusions to TRK inhibitors; RET fusions to selpercatinib; HER2 3+ to fam-trastuzumab deruxtecan-nxki.22,23,27,29,31,32
Supportive and psychosocial care are integral components of management, with early referral to palliative care and distress management services recommended.124
Follow-up for patients with no evidence of clinical disease includes imaging every 6–12 months for up to 5 years.12

cards.text: CUP is a heterogeneous group of metastatic tumors with an unknown primary site, accounting for 2%–9% of all tumors.2,8

The initial workup should include a core needle biopsy (preferred) or FNA with cell block, immunohistochemistry (IHC) staining, microsatellite instability (MSI) or mismatch repair (MMR) testing, HER2 IHC, and biomarker testing using next-generation sequencing (NGS).1

Follow-up for patients with no evidence of clinical disease includes imaging every 6–12 months for up to 5 years.12
